evidence: Vrijen described himself as becoming a '1% or 0.1% power user' of products—someone who dives so deep into a platform that he reaches functionality most users never discover. Yet he simultaneously identifies as 'non-technical'.
Vrijen has moved 12 cities across 33 years. 'Perhaps frequent relocation doesn't just make you adaptable—it makes you good at noticing adaptation.'

source_display: Human Support Systems vs Human-Centered AI
evidence: Zain's entire thesis centers on 'human support systems'—infrastructure needed to navigate an AI-automated future. 'How do we ensure that as we have more automation in workplace, as we see more AI and adoption, how do we ensure that's actually good for us as humans.'

source_display: Small World Algorithm as Trust Mechanism
evidence: The Logan connection—Zain randomly connected with Vrijen's co-founder on TikTok. 'Maybe our trust algorithms are broken. We optimize for efficiency, but trust is created by inefficiency.'
Vrijen stated 'there is zero way you could pay for my integrity because my belief in a product I like is too strong.' Integrity as a value floor that exceeds conventional market pricing.

Vrijen using AI to 'compensate for those gaps' (bad memory, rashness)—operating on negative space, compensating for what he lacks rather than enhancing what he has. 'AI as cognitive prosthesis, not enhancement.'
The Zo-TikTok-Logan overlap as evidence that network graphs have reached collision density. Strategy shifts from targeted outreach to graph density building.
Both agreed to swap contact details for scheduling coffee. Vrijen offered to share Zo source code and recommend Zain for FOHE membership.
Zain had randomly connected with Logan on TikTok after seeing her content about work and skills. 'Glitch in the matrix' moment during the call.
'We're not here to replace your product. We're the missing layer that makes your product defensible in the long run.' Careerspan as infrastructure layer for recruiting ecosystem.
Handshake has 1,600 universities but no way to capture behavioral signal. 'Real angle is data supremacy.' Selling opportunity to build proprietary dataset competitors can't replicate.
Positioning Careerspan as strategic hedge for Handshake's declining core business ($200M, only 6% growth). Honest positioning that reduces friction.
'Access without signal is noise. Employers have 20 million profiles with no way to identify who will succeed.' Behavioral signal more valuable than profile data.
'We're a three-person team. Like we're not even that expensive essentially to like to bring on.' Lean structure as acquisition asset.
Emphasis on month-on-month retention and return rates. 'Real value isn't in helping someone get one job—it's in tracking their entire career trajectory.'
Extended discussion about Handshake partnership opportunity. Core recruiting business ~$200M but only 6% growth. Looking for foothold in elite talent.
Pipeline review of multiple acquirer conversations. 'Thread the needle of being opportunistic but not just showing them as an opportunity, us not being desperate.'
'I'm actually about to be announced as co lead for nyc.' Offering to introduce Zain to FOHE community.
Anna explicitly requested shift: 'I want to do less of the logistics this year.' Vrijen agreed to take over event coordination responsibilities.
'20% increase over 90 days is material for the fact that I have not been posting.' Logan: 'I've gotten 2000 followers in the last 36 hours.' Organic demand for educational careers content.
